overrides.override_summary: Post-term covenant not to compete may not be enforceable under California law
overrides.why_it_matters: California broadly prohibits non-compete clauses; the 24-month post-term restriction is likely void for CA franchisees

overrides.override_summary: Binding arbitration in Colorado — franchisees encouraged to consult counsel on applicability of CA law and Federal Arbitration Act
overrides.why_it_matters: CA may override the Colorado venue requirement for arbitration/litigation

overrides.override_summary: Colorado choice of law may not be enforceable under California law
overrides.why_it_matters: California Franchise Investment Law may govern instead of Colorado law

overrides.override_summary: General release at renewal/transfer cannot waive rights under CA Franchise Investment Law (Corp Code §31512) or Franchise Relations Act (Bus & Prof Code §20010)
overrides.why_it_matters: Franchisees retain right to pursue claims under CA franchise law even after signing release

overrides.override_summary: Bankruptcy termination provision may not be enforceable under federal bankruptcy law (11 USC §101 et seq.)
overrides.why_it_matters: Franchisor cannot automatically terminate on bankruptcy filing

overrides.override_summary: Transfer and renewal releases must preserve rights under Hawaii Franchise Investment Law
overrides.why_it_matters: Non-waiver provisions of Hawaii law override standard release language

overrides.override_summary: If franchise agreement is inconsistent with Hawaii Franchise Investment Law on non-renewal/termination/transfer, Hawaii law controls
overrides.why_it_matters: Hawaii law provides additional protections on termination, non-renewal, and transfer

overrides.override_summary: Any waiver of claims under Illinois Franchise Disclosure Act is void (815 ILCS 705/41)
overrides.why_it_matters: Release cannot extinguish IL franchise law claims

overrides.override_summary: Illinois law governs the franchise relationship; jurisdiction in Illinois courts
overrides.why_it_matters: Colorado venue/law may not apply for IL franchise law claims

overrides.override_summary: General release does not apply to any liability under Maryland Franchise Registration and Disclosure Law
overrides.why_it_matters: MD franchise law claims survive any general release signed at renewal, sale, or transfer

overrides.override_summary: Franchisee may bring lawsuit in Maryland for claims arising under MD Franchise Law
overrides.why_it_matters: Franchisee not bound to Colorado venue for MD law claims

overrides.override_summary: 3-year statute of limitations from grant of franchise for MD Franchise Law claims
overrides.why_it_matters: Overrides the shorter 1-year/2-year limitation in the Franchise Agreement

overrides.override_summary: Termination only for good cause with up to 30-day cure; fair market value compensation on non-renewal (if term < 5 years)
overrides.why_it_matters: Michigan prohibits unfair termination provisions and requires compensation on non-renewal under certain conditions

overrides.override_summary: Cannot require litigation outside Michigan; cannot preclude entering into arbitration agreement at time of dispute
overrides.why_it_matters: Colorado venue requirement void in Michigan

overrides.override_summary: Cannot require assent to release that deprives franchisee of rights under Michigan Franchise Law
overrides.why_it_matters: Franchisees retain Michigan statutory rights regardless of releases signed

overrides.override_summary: 90-day notice of termination (with 60-day cure) and 180-day notice of non-renewal required (Minn. Stat. §80C.14)
overrides.why_it_matters: Substantially longer notice/cure periods than the 10/30-day periods in the FA

overrides.override_summary: No prospective general release of claims subject to Minnesota Franchise Act (Minn. Rule 2860.4400D)
overrides.why_it_matters: Cannot require general release at renewal/transfer for MN law claims

overrides.override_summary: Cannot require litigation outside Minnesota (Minn. Stat. §80C.21, Minn. Rule 2860.4400J)
overrides.why_it_matters: Colorado venue requirement void; MN law rights preserved

overrides.override_summary: NSF fee capped at $30 (Minn. Stat. §604.113)
overrides.why_it_matters: Overrides the FA's 5%/$50 NSF fee provision

overrides.override_summary: Transfer/renewal releases must preserve rights under NY General Business Law §§680–695
overrides.why_it_matters: NY statutory protections cannot be waived by general release

overrides.override_summary: Choice of law/forum provisions do not waive NY GBL rights (§§687.4, 687.5)
overrides.why_it_matters: NY franchisees retain NY law protections regardless of Colorado choice of law

overrides.override_summary: Covenants restricting competition contrary to NDCC §9-08-06 generally considered unenforceable
overrides.why_it_matters: The 24-month post-term noncompete is likely void in North Dakota

overrides.override_summary: Arbitration/mediation site must be agreeable to all parties, not remote from franchisee's business; jurisdiction/venue outside ND void
overrides.why_it_matters: Colorado venue requirement void; arbitration must be local to franchisee

overrides.override_summary: General release on renewal determined unfair, unjust, inequitable (NDCC §51-19-09)
overrides.why_it_matters: Cannot require general release as condition of renewal

overrides.override_summary: Choice of law requiring non-ND law is unfair/inequitable under NDCC §51-19-09
overrides.why_it_matters: ND law may apply instead of Colorado law

overrides.override_summary: No consent to termination or liquidated damages required from ND franchisees; jury trial waiver determined unfair
overrides.why_it_matters: ND franchisees retain right to jury trial and cannot be bound to liquidated damages consent

overrides.override_summary: Forum selection outside RI void for claims under RI Franchise Investment Act (R.I. Gen. Law §19-28.1-14)
overrides.why_it_matters: RI franchisees may litigate RI Act claims in Rhode Island

overrides.override_summary: General release void for claims under RI Franchise Investment Act
overrides.why_it_matters: RI statutory claims survive any release

overrides.override_summary: Termination without reasonable cause unlawful under VA Retail Franchising Act (§13.1-564)
overrides.why_it_matters: Non-curable default triggers may be unenforceable if they don't constitute 'reasonable cause' under VA law

overrides.override_summary: Arbitration/mediation must be in Washington or mutually agreed at time of dispute
overrides.why_it_matters: Colorado venue overridden; arbitration must be accessible to WA franchisee

overrides.override_summary: Release cannot include rights under WA Franchise Investment Protection Act except in negotiated settlement with independent counsel
overrides.why_it_matters: Standard release language void; WA Act rights preserved

overrides.override_summary: Noncompete void for employees earning <$100K/year and independent contractors earning <$250K/year (RCW 49.62.020/.030, adjusted for inflation)
overrides.why_it_matters: WA earnings thresholds may void noncompete for most caregivers and staff; franchise-level noncompete may also be subject to challenge

overrides.override_summary: Transfer fees collectible only to extent they reflect franchisor's reasonable estimated or actual costs
overrides.why_it_matters: The $10K/$20K transfer fees may be reduced if they exceed actual costs

overrides.override_summary: Wisconsin Fair Dealership Law (Title XIV-A Ch. 135) may affect termination provisions
overrides.why_it_matters: WI law provides additional protections against unfair termination of dealership/franchise relationships
